heroHeadline: SAF Recaptures Muqja Station in Blue Nile as Pakistan Suspends $1.5B Arms Deal; Drone Strikes Kill 3 at El Geneina Hospital
heroSubtitle: Sudan's Armed Forces retook the strategically vital Muqja station between Kurmuk and Ed Damazin on April 21, the first SAF counter-advance in Blue Nile State since RSF-SPLM-N captured Kurmuk on March 23 — slowing the threat to the Roseires Dam; separately, Pakistan suspended a $1.5 billion JF-17/drone arms package to SAF after Saudi Arabia withdrew financing under Western pressure, a blow to SAF air-power modernization; a drone struck the Sultan Medical Complex in RSF-held El Geneina killing 3; and community violence in Um Zaifa (South Darfur) killed 17 as inter-communal order continues to collapse under RSF occupation
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan, Radio Tamazuj, Amnesty International, Africanews, Wikipedia (Sudan Civil War Timeline 2026), NBC News, Middle East Eye, Deutsche Welle, Daily Pakistan, The Star (Reuters), AllAfrica
